channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UK gives sovereignty of the Chagos Islands to Mauritius. The US base on Diego Garcia stays
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government agreed Thursday to hand sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands, an archipelago of more than 60 islands in the Indian Ocean, to Mauritius, in a deal to secure the future of a strategically important U.K.-U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korean leader Kim Jong Un threatened to use nuclear weapons and destroy South Korea permanently if provoked, state media reported Friday, after the South’s leader warned that Kim’s regime would collapse if he attempted to use nuclear arms.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Boris Johnson says Trump presidency would have prevented Ukraine invasion
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson suggested that Donald Trump's presidency would have prevented Russia from invading Ukraine in 2022.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil's proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know as Israel expands its operations in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli airstrikes hammered targets across southern Lebanon and Beirut's suburbs while its military urged residents to flee more than two dozen Lebanese towns and villages, further signs that Israel is ramping up its operations in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At least 78 people died when a boat with 278 on board capsized in eastern Congo, official says
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — At least 78 people died when an overcrowded boat capsized on Lake Kivu in eastern Congo on Thursday, a local governor said. A frantic search and rescue operation was underway hours later as many remained unaccounted for from the vessel, believed to have had 278 people on board.
item.dc:creator: RUTH ALONGA, JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Ukrainian stronghold Vuhledar falls to Russian offensive after two years of bombardment
item.description: Vuhledar fell to Russian troops this week after Ukrainian forces were forced to withdraw to preserve lives and military equipment after more than two years of bombardment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum called “deplorable.”.
item.dc:creator: Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The Harpole Treasure includes one of the most valuable pieces of ancient jewelry found in Britain
item.description: The Harpole Treasure was discovered in 2022, and refers to a collection of valuable relics unearthed from a burial ground in England. Among the finds was a unique piece of jewelry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US Navy shows destroyer firing interceptor at Iranian missile
item.description: The Department of Defense confirmed two U.S. naval ships intercepted and destroyed missiles launched by Iran on the eastern Mediterranean Sea on Tuesday. USS Bulkeley (DDG-84) and USS Cole (DDG-67) fired a “dozen interceptors” at missiles targeting Israel.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Victor Perahia, Holocaust survivor and president of French Union of Auschwitz Deportees, dies at 91
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Victor Perahia, the president of the French Union of Auschwitz Deportees and a Holocaust survivor, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Shooting near luxury Mexico resort leaves 1 dead, suspects flee on jet skis
item.description: A Mexican national was shot and killed Wednesday near a ritzy resort in Cancun before two suspects fled on jet skis, according to a local report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Georgian parliament speaker signs anti-LGBTQ law after president refuses to sign it
item.description: TBILISI, Georgia (AP) — The speaker of the Georgian parliament signed into a law Thursday a bill that severely curtails LGBTQ+ rights in the country and mirrors legislation adopted in neighboring Russia.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Typhoon Krathon makes landfall in Taiwan, packing fierce winds and torrential rain
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Typhoon Krathon made landfall Thursday in Taiwan's major port city of Kaohsiung, bringing torrential rains and fierce winds to the island's south. Trees were brought down by high winds and roads were flooded, prompting the closure of schools and businesses.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel extends evacuation warnings in Lebanon, signaling a wider offensive
item.description: The Israeli military has warned people to evacuate a city and other communities in southern Lebanon that are north of a U.N.-declared buffer zone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum keeps doing morning briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President Claudia Sheinbaum started her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning news briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel presses forward on two fronts and reports 8 combat deaths as fears of a wider war mount
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel pressed forward on two fronts Wednesday, pursuing a ground incursion into Lebanon against Hezbollah that left eight Israeli soldiers dead and conducting strikes in Gaza that killed dozens, including children.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel defies Biden in Lebanon, testing US support
item.description: The Israeli operation in Lebanon is exposing the failures of U.S. efforts to de-escalate the Middle East conflict, raising the likelihood of a full-scale war against Hezbollah and testing President Biden's support for Israel's expanding military campaigns.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Rwanda records at least 9 deaths from Marburg outbreak
item.description: Rwanda has declared a public outbreak of the Marburg virus, confirming nearly 30 infections and at least nine deaths as of the end of September.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Catholic reform debate launched by Pope Francis leans away from ordained roles for women
item.description: ROME (AP) — Debate was leaning away from allowing women to take on ordained roles in the Catholic Church as the second phase of Pope Francis’ reform project opened Wednesday with an agenda topped by calls for women to take up more positions of responsibility Francis presided over an opening Mass in
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel bans UN secretary-general over anti-Israel actions: 'Doesn't deserve to set foot on Israeli soil'
item.description: The United Nations has increasingly faced criticism over the past year for how officials have responded to and handled the conflicts in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police patrols heightened in Jewish communities ahead of holidays
item.description: Police patrols will be heightened in Jewish communities ahead of a set of important holidays in Judaism, police across the U.S. told The Hill on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, The Hill

item.title: 8 Israeli soldiers killed in Lebanon as Netanyahu says IDF engaged in 'tough war' with Hezbollah
item.description: Israel's military announced Wednesday that eight soldiers have died during "combat in southern Lebanon" as troops are wiping out Hezbollah infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ancient painting revealed in Egypt beneath layers of bird poop
item.description: During restoration of the Temple of Edfu, artwork was discovered beneath layers of bird poop, dust and soot covering the temple walls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Mexico's army seizes local police weapons in cartel heartland amid gunfights, violence
item.description: The Mexican army has seized the firearms of local police in the Sinaloa city of Culiacan as cartel violence has escalated, dozens have been killed in the streets and distrust in the police grows.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two predawn explosions rock the area around the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen but no one is injured
item.description: COPENHAGEN, Denmark (AP) — Three young Swedes were arrested Wednesday in connection with two predawn explosions that occurred in the vicinity of the Israeli Embassy in Copenhagen, prompting a nearby Jewish school to close for the day. Police said no one was injured.
item.dc:creator: JAN M. OLSEN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Iran missile barrage on Israel 'appears to have been defeated'
item.description: Israel telegraphed that an attack of this sort could result in retaliation against significant Iranian targets.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Noury, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Who were the 7 high-ranking Hezbollah officials killed over the past week?
item.description: The move left Lebanon and much of the Mideast in shock as Israeli officials celebrated major military breakthroughs.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US airstrikes on Syria kill 37 militants affiliated with extremist groups
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Two U.S. airstrikes in Syria killed 37 militants affiliated with the Islamic State group and an al-Qaeda-linked group, the U.S. military said Sunday. It said two of the dead were senior militants. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Reward offered for Iranian in alleged Bolton assassination plot
item.description: Iranian Vice-President Mohammad Javad Zarif rejected accusations that Iran has tried to assassinate government critics overseas.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump and Zelenskyy meet in New York as election holds high stakes for US support for Ukraine
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy met face-to-face with Donald Trump on Friday with public tensions rising between the two over Ukraine's defense against Russia's invasion and in the midst of the U.S. presidential election.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Prince Harry leading initiative to support Southern African youth
item.description: “These young adults are eager and capable of driving meaningful change, yet significant barriers still stand in their way.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation
